message.content: {"analysis":"I can see the run-length compression scheme: free blocks use POOL_FREE_HEADER(wosize) where wosize encodes contiguous free block count. In calc_pool_stats, `p += wh * Wosize_hd(hd)` skips contiguous free blocks, then `p += wh` advances past the current block. Let me read the actual pool_sweep implementation and understand the full logic.","plan":"Read the pool_sweep implementation which is the core of what was modified. Also look at the allocation code to understand how free blocks are traversed for allocation.","commands":[{"keystrokes":"grep -n 'pool_sweep' /app/ocaml/runtime/shared_heap.c\n","duration":0.5},{"keystrokes":"sed -n '430,600p' /app/ocaml/runtime/shared_heap.c\n","duration":0.5}]}
